title: Chile: COVID-19 weekly death rate by vaccination status
subtitle: Death rates are calculated as the number of deaths in each group, divided by the total number of people in this group. This is given per 100,000 people.
caption:
Chile: COVID-19 weekly death rate by vaccination status, All ages
Death rates are calculated as the number of deaths in each group, divided by the total number of people in this group. This is given per 100,000 people.

Weekly COVID-19 mortality in Chile from October 2021 through March 2023 shows a consistent hierarchy where death rates per 100,000 people are highest among those with zero or one vaccine dose and lowest among those with three or four doses. The vertical axis measures deaths per 100,000 people, ranging from 0 to 12, while the horizontal axis tracks time across 18 months. The red line, representing individuals with zero or one dose, reaches the chart's absolute maximum of nearly 12 deaths per 100,000 in early 2022, peaking slightly above the brown line for two doses, which hits approximately 11 during the same period. In contrast, the green line for three doses peaks at roughly 4 deaths per 100,000, and the blue line for four doses remains the lowest throughout, never exceeding 2 deaths per 100,000. Following the major surge in early 2022, all four lines drop significantly, falling below 2 deaths per 100,000 by mid-2022 and remaining clustered near the bottom of the chart for the remainder of the timeline. A smaller secondary rise occurs around July 2022, where the red and brown lines briefly climb to between 2 and 3, while the green and blue lines show only minor fluctuations. The mortality rate for the 'All ages' group is age-standardized to account for the different vaccination rates of older and younger people. Across the entire period, the gap between the highest and lowest vaccination groups remains visible, with the zero-or-one-dose group consistently recording higher weekly death rates than the three-or-four-dose groups. The data shows that higher vaccination doses coincide with lower weekly death rates at every point in the timeline.
